enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs on a Saturday morning about his previous day, which included work and a visit to his mom's house. He discusses the difficulty of estimating project timelines for his ski resort website work, reflects on his plan to finish the Boulder project, and talks about leg pain from sitting too long.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Gave up trying to predict how long things take; better to decide how long you can work and just go
Must finish it regardless — staying another year if necessary despite leg pain

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday was Friday, not Saturday, November 11, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today is Saturday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday I had a pretty good day at work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got tired a little bit earlier at 10 or something yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got up at 7 yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to sleep at 11 last night.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to bed at 10 last night because I was kind of tired.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday I got up early, but I can't remember exactly when.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was going to leave my mom's house yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked and then I went to my mom's house yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to put some weight on my mom's windows yesterday, but I never got around to doing it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to put weight on my mom's windows today after work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to work on my paper again, my page, my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The ski resort thing is really dragging on because there's lots of stuff I didn't consider.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's really hard to predict how long it takes to do stuff.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Stuff always goes wrong and there's more to do than expected.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm giving up trying to predict how long it takes to do stuff.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Instead of predicting how long it takes to do stuff, it's best to just decide how long you can work on something and then just go as long as you can.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I could probably finish the ski resort thing if I go another few days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After finishing the ski resort thing, I might do circus level stuff.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I really need to finish Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Boulder is like an order to not solve it, I left the works.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My legs are starting to hurt.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I should sit down so much, it's like squashed my hamstrings to the point where it squeezes the nerves or something.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Sitting too much makes my legs hurt, like you want to walk around or lay in bed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The leg pain started like two weeks ago.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I started sleeping, laying on that couch.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm not so much on my couch in my room, because it's eight, but my leg on the table on it, pinched my legs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's kind of a kind of under-me that I'm in, because it's going to be difficult to go another year physically, because of my legs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't get my website done this year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There's no way I can finish my website this year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to need another year, a whole another year, but I think I can finish it another year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I guess I'll just have to buckle up and deal with some pain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Maybe I could just exercise a little bit more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I guess we'll be with my mom or son.
